macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Kashrut of Oil and the Framework of Repealed Rabbinic Decrees

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on the oil prohibition and its rabbinic nature
Brief discussion of cheese prohibition and its reasons (sakana vs. nevela)
Return to oil: the question of why it was prohibited and later permitted

macro_segments.title: Rav's Position: Daniel as Source of the Oil Prohibition

micro_segments.title: Rav's statement that Daniel made the gezeira against oil
Shmuel's alternative: seepage from tamei vessels as the concern
Gemara's challenge to Shmuel: not everyone maintains tahara
Shmuel's revised position: seepage of prohibited (non-kosher) vessels

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Defense and Rav's Justification of the Daniel Connection

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's argument that his theory explains why oil could be repealed
Shmuel challenges Rav: how can an earlier gezeira be repealed if Daniel made it?
Rav's defense against the principle of ein beit din yachol levatel
Rav's counter-attack: dismissing Simlai of Lod as unreliable
Rav's embarrassment and pivot to substantive defense via tradition

macro_segments.title: Rav's Scriptural Support from Daniel and the Personal vs. Binding Decree Question

micro_segments.title: Rav cites Daniel 1:8 as evidence for oil avoidance tradition
Rav admits the drashah is weak without prior tradition
Dispute between Rav and Shmuel on whether Daniel's decree was personal or binding
Shmuel's interpretation: Daniel decided for himself, not for all Israel

macro_segments.title: The Eighteen Decrees of Beit Shammai and Whether Oil Was Among Them

micro_segments.title: Question: was the oil prohibition among the eighteen decrees of Beit Shammai?
Possibility that Daniel tried the decree but it was rejected until later acceptance
Alternative: Daniel decreed only in urban areas; students extended to rural areas
Challenge: how could Rabbi Yehuda HaNasi repeal a decree of Beit Shammai and Hillel?

macro_segments.title: The Exception for Laws Not Accepted by the Majority: Oil as a Case Study

micro_segments.title: Principle from Rabbah bar bar Chana: all decrees can be repealed except the eighteen
Even Eliyahu in Messianic times cannot repeal the eighteen decrees
Rav Mesharshiya's explanation: the eighteen spread widely; oil did not
Rabbi Shmuel bar Abba's teaching: oil was investigated and found not widely accepted
Reliance on Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel and Rabbi Elazar bar Tzadok: majority must uphold

macro_segments.title: The Scriptural Source for the Majority Acceptance Principle

micro_segments.title: Last line of daf: Rav Adda bar Ahava's proof from Malachi 3:9
Verse requires entire nation for curse; applies to rabbinic decrees analogously
Discussion of conditions for accepting a decree with curse

macro_segments.title: The Four Categories and Their Daughters: Bread, Oil, Wine, and Daughters

micro_segments.title: Avimi's teaching: four things (bread, oil, wine, daughters) among the eighteen decrees
Question: what is meant by 'daughters' of these four items?
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: non-Jewish women considered niddot from infancy
Gneiva's position: all four categories due to avodah zarah concerns

macro_segments.title: Causal Chain: Bread, Oil, Wine, and Their Daughters Connected to Avodah Zarah

micro_segments.title: Bread prohibited because of oil; oil and bread because of wine
Wine prohibited because of daughters (non-Jewish women)
Daughters prohibited for some other reason (davar acher)
Additional davar acher for the davar acher (unclear reference)

macro_segments.title: Intermarriage Prohibitions: From Torah to Rabbinic Extensions

micro_segments.title: Non-Jewish women and marriage prohibitions: original seven nations vs. all idolaters
Rabbi Shimon ben Yochai: concern of turning away extends to all idolatrous women
Biblical law applies to marriage (chatnut); rabbinic extension to casual relations (znut)
Non-Jewish man with Jewish woman: Torah prohibition vs. Jewish man with non-Jewish woman

macro_segments.title: Historical Progression of Yichud Prohibitions from David through Beit Shammai and Hillel

micro_segments.title: Chashmonaim beit din prohibited sexual relations (biah) with non-Jewish women in private
Distinction: Chashmonaim prohibited biah but not yichud with non-Jewish women
David's beit din established yichud prohibition with Jewish women
Beit Shammai and Hillel extended yichud prohibition to non-Jewish women

macro_segments.title: The Halachic Status of Yichud and Its Prohibition

micro_segments.title: Yichud with Jewish woman is de'oraisa (but source is only a remez from Torah)
Rabbi Yochanan: remez to yichud from Deuteronomy regarding brother and sister
Son can be secluded with mother; others not with any arayot

topical_tags.term: ein beit din yachol levatel divrei beit din chaveiro (court cannot repeal predecessor's ruling)
eighteen decrees of Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel
lo pashat issuro berov Yisrael (decree did not spread among majority)
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel and Rabbi Elazar bar Tzadok on majority principle
four categories: bread, oil, wine, daughters
avodah zarah (idolatry) as reason for decrees
Pinchas and sexual prohibition with non-Jewish women
Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel on yichud extension
